key_insight: PBE-based machine-learning interatomic potentials can incorrectly predict HfO2’s ground state, overstabilizing a low-density I41/amd phase over the experimentally known monoclinic P21/c structure.
engineering_relevance: This shows that MLIP predictions for crystal stability and phase-transition barriers can inherit systematic DFT functional errors, affecting materials screening and ferroelectric device design.
method: Compared a PBE-trained MLIP against known HfO2 polymorphs.
Checked whether multiple PBE-based foundation models reproduce the same phase-ordering error.
Benchmarked different DFT functionals to trace the source of the instability ranking.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that both a PBE-based MLIP and widely used foundation models (NequIP-OAM-L, MatterSim-v1-5M) predict I41/amd as more stable than the real ground state, and that the error is reduced by PBEsol and LDA.
watch_item: Do not assume MLIP phase energetics are reliable if the underlying training labels come from a functional with known structural bias, especially for phases with large lattice relaxation.
paper_title: A wrong ground-state structure of HfO$_2$ predicted by machine-learning interatomic potentials based on the PBE functional
